_meta.note: ILLUSTRATIVE SYNTHETIC RULES for a hackathon POC. These are simplified stand-ins for real payment-integrity edits (NCCI PTP, LCD/NCD medical-necessity policy, plan payment policy). They are NOT production coding logic and must not be used for real adjudication.
_meta.generated_for: Cotiviti Agentic AI intern challenge - Topic 2 (Agentic decisioning for TPO)

ncci_edits.rationale: 80048 (Basic Metabolic Panel) is a component of 80053 (Comprehensive Metabolic Panel). Reporting both unbundles the panel; only the comprehensive panel is payable.

Edit pair between paring of benign hyperkeratotic lesion (11055) and nail debridement (11720). A distinct-service modifier (59 or X{EPSU}) permits separate payment when the services are performed at distinct anatomic sites and documented.

medical_necessity.rationale: Arthrocentesis/injection of a major joint (20610) is supported by osteoarthritis, joint effusion, bursitis, or inflammatory arthropathy diagnoses.

Transthoracic echocardiography (93307) is supported by heart failure, valvular disease, arrhythmia, cardiomyopathy, or cardiac-symptom diagnoses. A routine wellness visit alone does not establish medical necessity.

99213.description: Established patient office visit, low-moderate complexity

99214.description: Established patient office visit, moderate complexity

99215.description: Established patient office visit, high complexity

36415.description: Collection of venous blood by venipuncture

modifier_reference.25: Significant, separately identifiable E/M service by the same physician on the same day of a procedure.
modifier_reference.59: Distinct Procedural Service - identifies procedures not normally reported together but appropriate under the circumstances.

upcoding_watch.rationale: A high-complexity E/M code billed with only a routine/low-acuity encounter diagnosis is a possible up-coding signal. It cannot be confirmed from claim data alone and requires medical-record review.

unbundling.text: National Correct Coding Initiative Procedure-to-Procedure edits prevent unbundling, where a comprehensive service is fragmented into its component parts to increase reimbursement. When a column-2 code is a component of a column-1 code, the column-2 code is not separately payable unless an approved modifier indicates a distinct service.

medical_necessity.text: A service is medically necessary when it is consistent with the diagnosis and accepted standards of care. Coverage determinations link procedure codes to supporting diagnosis codes; a procedure billed without a supporting diagnosis may be denied for lack of medical necessity.

duplicate.text: A duplicate claim reports the same service, for the same member, by the same provider, on the same date of service as a previously adjudicated claim. Duplicates are non-payable to prevent double payment.

high_dollar_review.text: Governance policy: any automated denial exceeding the high-dollar threshold, or any decision below the confidence threshold, must be routed to a human reviewer before final disposition. Autonomous agents may recommend but not finalize such actions.
